# LineBot — Instruction File

> **Author:** Vishal Narayanan  
> **Generated:** 2026-07-15 15:07  
> **Platform:** ROS2 Jazzy + Arduino Uno  

---

## 1. Project Overview

**Description:** A differential-drive robot that follows a black line on a white or non-black surface.

---

## 2. Hardware Configuration

| Component        | Details |
|------------------|---------|
| Line Sensor Type | 3x single TCRT5000 |
| Sensor Channels  | 3 |
| Motor Driver     | L298N |
| Motors           | DC gear motors 200 RPM |
| Power Supply     | 4x AA batteries |
| Arduino ↔ ROS2   | USB Serial |

---

## 3. ROS2 Setup

| Setting          | Value |
|------------------|-------|
| Distribution     | Jazzy |
| Host OS          | Ubuntu (latest version) |
| Workspace        | `linebot_ws` |
| Package Name     | `line_follower` |

### Key Topics

- `/sensor_data`
- `/cmd_vel`
- `/line_error`
- `/odm`

---

## 4. Arduino Firmware

| Setting            | Value |
|--------------------|-------|
| ROS2 Library       | micro_ros_arduino or rosserial_arduino |
| Control Algorithm  | PID |
| PID Parameters     | TBD |

---

## 5. Behaviour & Features

| Setting        | Value |
|----------------|-------|
| Line / Surface | Black line on white surface |
| Target Speed   | 0.2 m/s, 150 PWM units |

**Extra Features:**

ROS2 dashboard, logging,  intersection detection

---

## 6. Quick-Start Steps

```bash
# 1. Flash Arduino firmware
#    Open the .ino sketch in Arduino IDE and upload to the Uno.

# 2. Source ROS2
source /opt/ros/jazzy/setup.bash

# 3. Build the workspace
cd ~/linebot_ws
colcon build --symlink-install
source install/setup.bash

# 4. Launch the line follower
ros2 launch line_follower line_follower.launch.py
```
